reachability_artifacts: go test ./internal/driver/... -v output: TestRoleSet (4 subtests), TestAssertWorktree (5 subtests incl. linked-worktree), TestNoWireImports — all PASS

effort_complexity.rationale: Small diff, keystone contract — shape errors here are the release's most expensive defect class.

design_decisions.choice: Driver contract shape: role-dispatch with declared RoleSet (Dispatch(ctx, DispatchInput) -> Result; capability IS the role set, checked fail-fast at resolution; engine validates Result.StructuredJSON against verifier-verdict-v1 fail-closed; wire types internal to in-process drivers)
options: minimal Dispatch core + optional interfaces discovered by type-assert (today's RunAgentic pattern generalised)
maximal: fold non-loop utility judgements (gates/bench) into Dispatch too
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad, 2026-07-02, planning session (AskUserQuestion decision card; recorded in intake.md 'Driver contract shape: role-dispatch'). Companion clauses decided in the same session: role-universality (any driver may serve any loop role it declares), one-shot model.Verifier survives utility-only, explicit-table registration with enumeration API, explicit prefix->driver resolution with no smart fallback.
design_decisions.rationale: Matches role-universality exactly; kills the sworn#35 advertises-but-ignores class at resolution time instead of runtime; accepted cost is the largest rewire of the verify.go/slice.go seams (T4). Silent-reroute fallbacks rejected per the sworn#69 lesson.
